qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: ForwardAuth unbounded auth-response buffering can cause request-driven memory exhaustion
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is an HTTP ForwardAuth memory-exhaustion path in pkg/config/dynamic/middlewares.go and pkg/middlewares/auth/forward.go where an unset maxResponseBodySize becomes -1 and the auth response body is fully buffered with io.ReadAll. The advisory is a distinct gRPC authorization-bypass root cause triggered by malformed :path values without a leading slash in the grpc-go dependency, causing deny-rule mismatches while routing still succeeds.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described gRPC deny-rule bypass. The only final qualifying finding is a separate ForwardAuth response-buffering denial-of-service issue.
evidence: The advisory JSON describes a remote, unauthenticated gRPC authorization bypass caused by malformed HTTP/2 :path values that omit the leading slash, so path-based deny rules evaluate the wrong non-canonical path while routing still succeeds.

The EvoHunt session manifest and execution history materialized one final qualifying finding: /Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_dfc4ec85/forwardauth-unbounded-response-buffering-dos. The example/ directory is session scaffolding and was not listed as a final vulnerability folder.

That finding's report, checklist, and code references point to ForwardAuth configuration and runtime code in pkg/config/dynamic/middlewares.go and pkg/middlewares/auth/forward.go, where an unset MaxResponseBodySize defaults to -1 and readResponseBodyBytes uses io.ReadAll on the auth response body before the allow or deny decision.

The advisory-specific dependency surface is google.golang.org/grpc in artifacts/upstream-check/go.mod, but the qualifying finding does not reference malformed gRPC :path handling, deny-rule matching, or the grpc-go authorization path at all.
